source: Apollo People Match enrichment + agent-drafted cold email
activationNote: Auto-send is ARMED and active. The daily auto-send scheduled task (id 30886b35, 9 AM ET, background) was created on 2026-07-04 after explicit user launch approval. It will send up to 10 drafted emails/day via Gmail, starting with the next 9 AM ET fire. Day 1 sends 10 leads, Day 2 sends the last 2. To pause, delete cron 30886b35 — this flag stays true but no task means no sends.

guards.idempotentSendState: status flips drafted -> sending -> sent to prevent duplicate sends on retry
guards.optOutLine: If this isn't relevant, reply 'not interested' and I won't follow up.
guards.sendingOnlyInScheduledAgent: The Express app and published site display state only; sends happen inside the scheduled task.

sentLog.subject: CRE outreach ops — an AI pilot worth a look
Lightening the outreach load for a digital health team
Scaling BD outreach without scaling headcount
Keeping your sales pipeline on autopilot
Less pipeline busywork for your consulting practice
You build email automation — here's the outbound side
Freeing up your team's outreach bandwidth

leads.name: David Lee
leads.title: President
leads.subject: CRE outreach ops — an AI pilot worth a look
leads.body:
Hi David,

In commercial real estate, the bottleneck is usually follow-up — sourcing the right contacts, personalizing outreach to brokers and owners, and tracking who actually replied. I'm piloting an AI agent with a few teams that handles that pipeline end to end: it researches prospects, drafts the outreach, and triages replies so nothing falls through.

Curious if that'd save your team a few hours a week. Worth a quick call to compare notes?

Best,
Jackson Vanderpool

If this isn't relevant, reply 'not interested' and I won't follow up.

Would it be worth a short call to see if this fits how your team works?
